title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · Graph Lens: Gita Multi Remote Publication
summary: A mirror, fork, upstream, deployment remote, and namespace-specific integration ref disagree after a rewrite; one stale client may overwrite reviewed work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita multi remote publication increment in distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.
Use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Multi Remote Publication
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G14 is a theory in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Multi Remote Publication contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture remote urls, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new ids, server result, and post-fetch verification.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Multi Remote Publication while producing distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G14 is a worked example in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push. Competency probe: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification. A defensible solution applies Gita Multi Remote Publication, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-multi-remote-publication — Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Multi Remote Publication evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-multi-remote-publication-l8c2p" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Multi Remote Publication into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-multi-remote-publication-l8c2p
# scenario-key: trace-l8c2p probe-p2c8l boundary-c2pl8 owner-l8c2px fault-l8c2pz
# before-ref: main=a11cel8 candidate=b00b12p
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11cel8 candidate=c0ffeep
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs

content.title: Evidence defense
